title: Package Quality and Brief Audit
scientificQuestion: Can route policy v3 scale from two target classes to at least four class-level acceleration candidate classes while preserving route quality and public package quality?
domain: Cross-Domain Evidence Routing OS route policy v3 scale
limitations: The run uses a bounded blind workload and does not measure open-ended real-world throughput.
Class-level status is limited to target classes represented in this workload.
Public packages are minimum-evidence packages, not full expert review.
Repo, temporal, and formal classes remain below the class-level threshold in this run.
Time-to-evidence baselines are conservative estimates and remain benchmark artifacts.
No outside use or outside confirmation is claimed.
disclaimer: Bounded route-policy-v3 scale workload result. It reports target-class evidence only; it does not claim broad acceleration, outside uptake, prize significance, broad autonomous intelligence, medical or legal authority, hazardous capability, or universal validity.
productChangeJustification: No Product change was needed because route_policy_v3 already exposes class-specific thresholds, confidence calibration, package-quality scoring, and class-level scoring used by this scale run.
